title: betula verrucosa subsp. obscura
intro: Betula verrucosa subsp. obscura, also known as the dark-leaved birch, is a subspecies of the paper birch tree, characterized by its dark green, serrated leaves and white, papery bark. The medicinal properties of this plant include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been used to treat a variety of health issues, including skin conditions and wounds. The health benefits of Betula verrucosa subsp. obscura include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve skin health, and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be used to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, and to promote wound healing.
